item.title: Beloved central Queensland theatre-restaurant sells up after 32 years in the biz
item.description: One of Queensland's last "dinner and a show" venues is selling up, following a long-running decline of theatre-restaurants across the country.

item.title: First Australian hantavirus cruise ship passenger speaks from quarantine
item.description: The first Australian to speak publicly about being onboard the MV Hondius shares his experience of the deadly outbreak on the ship, as he prepares to leave a Perth quarantine facility and return home to his family.

item.title: Mexican military shoots down drone at South Korea's World Cup training facility
item.description: Sources confirm Mexican authorities shot down a drone at South Korea's training facility as reports of suspect aircraft rise.

item.title: After years of reform, a preventable disease eradicated in 'white Australia' is still on the rise
item.description: Years after losing his sister to a preventable disease, a grieving Gulf of Carpentaria man is demanding urgent action to address a disease found almost exclusively in remote, Indigenous communities.

item.title: Bird flu kills thousands of baby seals on Heard Island in Southern Ocean
item.description: Research has revealed the devastating impact of bird flu on a remote Australian territory where more than 13,000 baby seals are now believed to have been killed by the virus.

item.title: Great Southern farmer combines love of farming and filmmaking to put her region in the spotlight
item.description: Farmer Brianna Jones has worked on some of Western Australia's biggest film productions, such as Blueback, Sweet As, and The Surfer.

item.title: Lauren Day explains terms of US-Iran ceasefire deal
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Donald Trump says he's signed the Iran memorandum
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Donald Trump signs Iran memorandum at Versailles
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ABC meteorologist Nate Byrne answers your questions about El Niño – as it happened
item.description: With the Bureau of Meteorology formally declaring an El Niño event, two experts unpack what this will mean for Australia's weather in the coming months.

item.title: SA government's $28m fund for farming assistance not used for a decade
item.description: A South Australian government fund of more than $28 million for loans and grants for farmers has not been used for the past 10 years, despite the industry struggling through drought and poor market conditions.

item.title: Victoria's crime wave shows signs of falling, according to latest crime statistics
item.description: Figures for the year to the end of March show youth crime fell by 6 per cent in Victoria, but adult crime rose by around 10 per cent.

item.title: New lease with Morris Group announced for Double Island off Cairns coast
item.description: A new lease has been announced for the abandoned Double Island resort, and Brad Pitt, Keanu Reaves and Jennifer Aniston have been invited back for the reopening party.

item.title: Albanese and Chalmers unveil capital gains carve-outs for small businesses, startups
item.description: The federal government has consulted with small businesses and startups over contentious elements of its budget tax proposal.

item.title: Three pet dogs dead after suspected baiting in Echuca
item.description: The dogs died within hours of eating an unknown substance while being walked at a trotting track in a regional Victorian town.

item.title: Snowtown serial killer James Vlassakis's release on parole subject to further review
item.description: Another review will be held to decide whether Snowtown serial killer James Vlassakis should be released on parole.

item.title: World Cup chaos could be concern for England after Croatia win
item.description: England's World Cup opener against Croatia, with a missed penalty and a flaky defence, seemed to be briefly polluted by fear.

item.title: Coroner finds 'no breakthrough' after inquest into death of Mark Haines
item.description: The mysterious death of Mark Haines, whose body was discovered on railway tracks in northern NSW in 1988, would have been treated differently "if it was a white boy", according to the coroner.

item.title: Teenager accused of stabbing Universal Store co-founder Greg Josephson to death committed to stand trial
item.description: A teenager accused of killing a 58-year-old man at a house party in Brisbane's north last year has been ordered to stand trial.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Ibrahamovic calls Fox Sports colleagues 'ignorant', Boston almost runs out of beer and Ronaldo struggles
item.description: Zlatan Ibrahimovic drops "ignorant" bomb to Fox Sports colleagues, Scotland's fans almost drink Boston dry and Awer Mabil gets starstruck in front of SBS legend David Basheer. Here are the quick hits from today at the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Key moments from Donald Trump's extraordinary Iran deal press conference
item.description: Donald Trump has held a press conference as the United States and Tehran have signed a memorandum of understanding to end the war in Iran.

item.title: Why Max Plath is the latest in a long line of Queensland State of Origin cult heroes
item.description: The players who fill the spaces in between the stars are the secret to Queensland's Origin dominance and in Max Plath the Maroons have found yet another player who knows just how to do it.

item.title: What are the knowns and unknowns about US-Iran Deal?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Locals fear one of the largest data centres proposed for Asia Pacific will use water from 'stressed' River Murray
item.description: A massive data centre proposed for SA faces scrutiny over water and environmental risks.

item.title: Union says Tahmoor sale could bring miners home after Gupta fallout
item.description: Hundreds of former Tahmoor miners could soon return to jobs close to home after liquidators announced the sale of the mine to a consortium led by Golden Energy and Resources and M Resources.

item.title: Global ransomware group reportedly claim cyber attack that shut down Mackay Sugar mills
item.description: A Russian-speaking ransomware operation known as The Gentlemen has reportedly claimed responsibility on the dark web for a cyber attack that shut down two regional Queensland sugar mills for a week.

item.title: Police investigating after geese mowed down by driver in Malmsbury
item.description: Police are investigating whether a driver deliberately mowed down a gaggle of geese crossing a road in central Victoria.

item.title: Data centre boom sparks warning to consider global location climate risks
item.description: As data centres rapidly come online to meet demand, governments are urged to ensure development does not outpace adequate planning.

item.title: Court hears woman bashed elderly neighbour, dumped body in river in Melbourne's west
item.description: Milena Bogojevska spent more than $4,000 she stole from the home of 85-year-old Lolene Whitehand, who she had fatally bashed then dumped in the Maribyrnong River.

item.title: Why the power of Press Club addresses is in the clips posted on social media afterwards
item.description: When Pauline Hanson stood up at the National Press Club, it was not just for the audience watching on broadcast TV. In the age of social media, the bite-sized clips that come later are far more important.

item.title: Sarah Game won't rule out introducing further bill to restrict late term abortion
item.description: Family First upper house MLC Sarah Game has not ruled out introducing another bill restricting late-term abortion access, after her proposed legislation to restrict abortion after 25 weeks failed in the lower house last night.

item.title: Ancient oak tree linked to the legend of Robin Hood in Sherwood Forest is dead, experts say
item.description: The tree is said to have sheltered Robin Hood, the legendary 13th-century bandit who stole from the rich and gave to the poor and took refuge in Nottingham Forest.

item.title: National Trust of SA referred to parliamentary committee over 'accountability and oversight' concerns
item.description: An outpouring of discontent among National Trust volunteers in recent months has led state parliament to inquire into "accountability and oversight" concerns within the major conservation group.

item.title: Australian War Memorial director says invite to Ben Roberts-Smith 'standard practice' ahead of Afghanistan gallery opening
item.description: Australian War Memorial director Matt Anderson is asked about an invitation issued to Ben Roberts-Smith, who has been accused of war crimes, to the upcoming opening of a new gallery space dedicated to Australia's service in Afghanistan.

item.title: Gina Rinehart gifts Pauline Hanson a symbolic orange bulldozer
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: $430m Murray-Darling water deal reignites food production, river health debate
item.description: A $430 million Murray-Darling Basin water buyback sparks a fresh political fight over the balance between food production and environmental recovery.

item.title: Greens leader quizzed on her comparison of Jeremy Rockliff with Joseph Stalin
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Magpies admit Bobby Hill will not make AFL return for some time
item.description: Bobby Hill returns to Collingwood training, but coach Craig McRae says it will be some time before the 2023 Norm Smith medallist is ready to play in the AFL again.

item.title: A growling Gina Rinehart gifts Pauline Hanson a 'beautiful' orange bulldozer
item.description: Four years ago, Australians decided they didn't want a bulldozer for a prime minister. But now if Gina Rinehart has her way, a big orange one will be ploughing her way through the political arena.

item.title: Pensioner living in car has 'lost faith' in system amid WA housing waitlist overhaul
item.description: Despite upcoming changes from the WA government to overhaul the housing waitlist, a 68-year-old living in his car says he has lost faith in the system as an advocate calls for faster action.

item.title: Coalition claims high migration has become new normal as Australia records 300,000 new arrivals
item.description: Net overseas migration remained above 300,000 last year, fuelling opposition claims Labor has locked in an unsustainable new normal despite the number of arrivals continuing to fall.

item.title: Giants not sweating on Toby Greene AFL contract extension talks
item.description: The Giants are keen to retain Toby Greene, but they are happy to wait until the 32-year-old is ready to make a call on his future.

item.title: Gina Rinehart says Queensland should give some of its islands to Elon Musk
item.description: The mining magnate, speaking at the National Bush Summit, also talked about how she believed Townsville could become a base for the Israeli military.

item.title: 'Missed' opportunities to support mental health of man fatally shot by police, inquest hears
item.description: The inquest heard Mr Donahue's mother had shared concerns about his mental health in the weeks leading up to his death.

item.title: Fashion boss Shmuel Tal faces trial over allegations he detained and raped woman in Byron Bay
item.description: A woman says she was scared to leave the Byron Bay holiday home of a Melbourne man, who she alleges forced her to engage in 11 non-consensual sex acts over a four hour-period.

item.title: Aussie dollar volatile after US Fed signals rates likely to rise
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Dockers down Cats as Bolton takes mark of the year contender
item.description: Fremantle comes from behind to beat Geelong for a 13th straight game, as Shai Bolton hits the heights at Perth Stadium with a Mark of the Year contender.

item.title: Crows legend Tony Modra seriously injured in truck crash near Victor Harbor
item.description: Crows legend Tony Modra has been seriously injured in a truck crash.

item.title: Volodomyr Zelenskyy says 'Moscow will burn' if Russian strikes continue
item.description: Scores of drones targeted Moscow overnight in what was Ukraine's largest attack on the Russian capital.

item.title: Horrific injury mars Canadian celebrations, Mexico secures knockout
item.description: Canada claims its first ever win at a World Cup, but celebrations against Qatar have been soured by a broken leg suffered by Ismaël Koné.

item.title: Man arrested after boy critically injured in crocodile enclosure at UK zoo
item.description: British police say they have arrested a man on suspicion of attempted murder after being called to a popular zoo north of London.

item.title: ABC news quiz: Soccer celebrations, bin battles, phone fails and an internet-famous duck
item.description: Today's news quiz is heating up like El Niño. Will you strut like an internet-famous duck or get schooled like Prince George?

item.title: Are old buildings the solution to new homes in a housing crisis?
item.description: From former post offices to pumping stations, there are buildings with commercial histories all around Australia. Could repurposing them into homes help with the housing supply?

item.title: Telco given warning after consumers lose millions in HSBC text scam
item.description: A global telco that failed to stop more than 1,000 scam text messages from reaching Australians has faced no consequences, the ABC can reveal.

item.title: US primary elections swamped with accusations of funny business
item.description: A Democrat is accused of being a Republican in disguise; a Republican is accused of being a Democrat plant. It is primaries season in the US, and suspicions of dirty pool are rife.

item.title: Pauline Hanson's views on paid parental leave outdated and concerning, advocates say
item.description: Pauline Hanson's openness to discuss a winding back of paid parental leave is concerning and harks back to an Australia that no longer exists, advocates say.

item.title: Cheat Sheet: Toy Story has changed animation every step of the way
item.description: The multi-billion-dollar franchise has overcome rewrites, accidental deletions and generations of changing tastes to bring Toy Story 5 to cinemas in 2026.

item.title: Proposed data centre in South Australia dividing locals who want the jobs but fear water demand
item.description: To its supporters, this proposed data centre will provide much-needed regional jobs, but in the nation's driest state, critics say the draw on river water to cool the machines could be too great.

item.title: AFL matriarch helped Dezi Freeman's wife get to Australia, stored family's guns
item.description: Kay Reid, the mother of premiership players Ben and Sam Reid, reveals that she and her husband Bruce's deep ties to police killer Dezi Freeman and his family date back two decades.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes residential building in Moscow
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Concerns raised about teacher training for special schools as allocations are unfulfilled
item.description: Data shows special schools across Queensland are operating with fewer than the number of allocated teachers.

item.title: The pros and cons of retiring overseas with your pension
item.description: With some foreign countries offering cheaper living expenses, it is no surprise that moving overseas to retire is increasingly attractive to some older Australians. But before you up stumps and book that flight, read this.

item.title: JD Vance warns critics over Iran deal and says Trump is Israel's only ally
item.description: US Vice-President JD Vance lashes out at Israeli critics of the Iran deal, saying Donald Trump is Israel's only ally, in a sharp rebuke that referenced the billions in American defence aid the country receives.

item.title: Auditor-general's report finds authorities missed targets ahead of Grampians bushfires
item.description: An auditor-general's report finds fire authorities missed planned fuel reduction targets before two disastrous fire seasons and warns properties could be at risk if strategic fuel breaks are not put in place.

item.title: Albanese government gives ground on tax, but how much will it have to cede on the NDIS?
item.description: The government's announced changes to its tax package are seeking to allay the fierce post-budget backlash from vested interests and some commentators.

item.title: Push to create 1.2-million-hectare Great Southern Forest across NSW South Coast
item.description: The National Parks Association wants an ambitious 1.2-million-hectare national park spanning from the Illawarra to the Victorian border.

item.title: How Socceroo goalkeeper Patrick Beach traded Western Sydney fields for World Cup centre stage
item.description: At just 22, Patrick Beach stunned the world in his FIFA World Cup debut and third international start with the Socceroos against Türkiye last weekend.

item.title: Adelaide fashion icon's death after taking magic mushrooms to be probed at inquest
item.description: The death of an Adelaide fashion industry icon who suffered a cardiac arrest after taking magic mushrooms as an alternative treatment will be probed at a coronial inquest.

item.title: Olive Eather's journey living with dementia on her family farm in central Queensland
item.description: After Olive Eather was diagnosed with dementia, the family farmstay changed its business model to keep her at home for longer.

item.title: Arthur Boyd tapestries of Saint Francis of Assisi on display after 50 years
item.description: Tapestries depicting the life of Saint Francis of Assisi will be displayed together for the first time since being commissioned in the late 1960s by Australian artist Arthur Boyd.

item.title: Sacred Victorian Indigenous site Lal Lal Falls runs dry as upstream 'greed' blamed
item.description: Indigenous and environmental groups are accusing a Chinese company of hoarding water on a Victorian property and causing a sacred waterfall to run dry.

item.title: China to impose 55 per cent tariff on Australian beef — as it happened
item.description: Australian beef exports to China will be slapped with an additional 55 per cent tariff from tomorrow, after annual shipment quotas were exceeded.

item.title: World Cup: Lionel Messi calls for 'humanity' from media as father receives medical treatment
item.description: Argentina superstar Lionel Messi and his family ask the media for "humanity" amid rumours about his father Jorge Messi's health.

item.title: KPMG whistleblower scandal an 'egregious breach of trust', ASIC chair says
item.description: A huge day in Australia's parliament, as an inquiry drills into a whistleblower scandal at the consultancy firm, KPMG. See how this unfurled on our live blog.

item.title: Australia's Rinky Hijikata stuns Jiri Lehecka at the Queen's Club Championships
item.description: Australia's Rinky Hijikata scores one of the wins of his career at the Queen's Club Championships.

item.title: WA mother says NDIS reforms will hit regional Australia hardest
item.description: Annette Nicholas and her husband last year became disability support workers to share their "lived experience" of raising 15-year-old son Tre, who is intellectually disabled and also lives with autism.

item.title: Alice Springs Beanie Festival celebrates 30 years of unique crafty creations
item.description: As the Alice Springs Beanie Festival celebrates its 30th and final year, we're exploring the weird and wonderful handmade creations — and the characters working hard behind the scenes — that have made the annual event so special.

item.title: Virgin extends travel date for expiring COVID-19 flight credits after criticism
item.description: Customers will still have to spend their COVID-19 flight credits credits by June 30, but will now have more time to travel.

item.title: JD Vance warns Israel against attacking Trump over US-Iran deal
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands reveals new subscription-based show as his next move after $12m ARN Media settlement
item.description: Radio shock jock Kyle Sandilands reveals the details of his next move after accepting a "piddly little" settlement of $12 million in his contract dispute with ARN Media.

item.title: Fuel storage lid flies off after Ukrainian drone strike on Moscow refinery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Report claims Coalition's renewable 'buffer zone' puts projects at risk
item.description: Energy experts have mixed views on what the Victorian Coalition's proposed renewable energy buffer zone policy could mean for the state's energy transition.

item.title: World Cup: Photographers may be ruining coaches' proudest moments during national anthems
item.description: Photographers have been allowed to get very close to the coaches during national anthems at the World Cup, but not everyone is happy.

item.title: Fluke, or are the Socceroos actually good?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Northern Territory Labor dumps Deputy Opposition Leader Dheran Young to win back trust
item.description: The Northern Territory Labor party has replaced its deputy opposition leader with a newcomer to parliament, in a move they say will win back the trust and confidence of the people of Darwin.

item.title: Cadia gold mine hits pause after two earthquakes felt across western NSW
item.description: A magnitude-3.4 earthquake within the Newmont Cadia Gold Mine site, south of Orange, is felt by people in surrounding areas.

item.title: David Quill shares story to help shed light on life with MND
item.description: After a career in forestry, David Quill uses woodworking to help his motor neurone disease fight.

item.title: Iranians left devastated as US signs deal with regime they hoped would fall
item.description: From inside Iran, citizens say they feel abandoned and forgotten as the United States signs a deal with the regime Washington launched a war against.

item.title: Wife of AFL legend Tony Modra thanks first responders following truck crash
item.description: The wife of AFL icon Tony Modra has expressed her gratitude to emergency services and hospital staff as the Crows legend remains in hospital in a "critical but stable condition" following a truck crash in South Australia.

item.title: What North Queensland thinks of Rinehart's idea of giving islands to Musk for satellite launches
item.description: The Townsville Enterprise chief executive says "all ideas are welcome" after mining magnate Gina Rinehart suggested islands off North Queensland's coast should be offered to SpaceX CEO Elon Musk for satellite launches.

item.title: Third person charged over fire that destroyed Adass Israel Synagogue in Ripponlea
item.description: Police have charged a 20-year-old from Melbourne's north-west with a number of charges including arson over a fire that destroyed the Adass Israel Synagogue in 2024.

item.title: Friends turn World Cup trip into tribute after mate's sudden death
item.description: The flights were booked for the trip of a lifetime for a group of best mates when one of them suddenly died.

item.title: Seasonal alpine staff seek alternative work amid lack of snow in NSW
item.description: The Snowy Mountains are not quite living up to their name as a slow start to the ski season leaves some seasonal workers out of a job.

item.title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election, poised to challenge Britain's PM Keir Starmer
item.description: Andy Burnham, a British Labour lifer, is expected to challenge Sir Keir Starmer for the country's top job after re-entering the House of Commons via a by-election.

item.title: WA government celebrates "thousands" of wildlife crossings on South West highway
item.description: The WA government has released night-vision images showing scores of native wildlife are using rope bridges and underpasses along a $1.46 billion highway in the South West region, but not everyone is convinced.

item.title: Twins Jack and Artie Porter hear their mum for the first time
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Move to prevent 'rivers of grog' as NT town camp alcohol bans to be extended
item.description: The NT government is extending alcohol bans in town camps beyond 2027, in a move welcomed by the health sector as a win for community safety.

item.title: Veteran prop James Slipper comes out of retirement for Wallabies at Nations Championship Tests
item.description: James Slipper comes out of retirement and three uncapped players are named for the Wallabies ahead of the Nations Championship Tests.

item.title: Live facial recognition technology to be used by WA Police in Australian first
item.description: WA Police are set to become the first in the country to trial live facial recognition of crowds, describing the technology as safer than standard CCTV cameras.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: A broken leg, fraying tempers and Brazil beware the 'Rocky Curse'
item.description: A horror injury and some fraying tempers sour an historic day for Canada as Brazil fans fear the 'Rocky Curse' in Philadelphia. Here are today's quick hits from the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Should police be able to scan your face while you're at the shops?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Pauline Hanson's claim businesses 'can't sack' workers gets mixed reaction from HR, small business
item.description: Pauline Hanson's claims that Australian businesses are "tied" to lazy workers they "can't sack", are labelled inaccurate by some employment law experts and small business advocates, while one HR representative says businesses are "terrified to terminate".

item.title: World Cup: The Socceroos have always believed, now the rest of the world is taking notice
item.description: When USA coach Mauricio Pochettino was asked about Australia's strengths ahead of the two sides' World Cup meeting in Seattle, he settled on a word — belief.

item.title: Salmon Tasmania fish pen ad banned over potentially misleading content
item.description: A Salmon Tasmania advertisement promoted across Facebook and in cinemas has been removed following a breach in advertising standards.

item.title: US accused of trying to 'edit out' climate change in Antarctic report
item.description: The US has been criticised over its reluctance to refer to "climate change" at a major Antarctic meeting, with France describing it as a "dangerous precedent" that risks undermining science.

item.title: Short on space? These are veggies you can grow indoors and on balconies
item.description: Even if you're short on outdoor space, there are ways to grow your own food at home. Here's what the experts recommend.

item.title: From a White House podium, JD Vance delivers 'stunning development' in US-Israel relationship
item.description: The US vice-president takes public aim at Israel with a series of comments Washington watchers say are "unprecedented".

item.title: Voters surprised by failure to vote notices from SA Electoral Commission
item.description: Multiple voters have received notices from the Electoral Commission of South Australia informing them they will be fined for not voting in the state election, despite having voted.

item.title: Suspected detection of H5 strain of bird flu in WA, which would be first in Australia
item.description: A wild migratory bird in Western Australia has returned a suspected positive result for avian influenza, which has devastated populations of seabirds, seals and other animals across the world.

item.title: NSW live music inquiry told lack of regional music education impacting talent pipeline
item.description: A NSW parliamentary inquiry into the state of live music has heard concerns about a lack of music education in schools and the knock-on effect on future events.

item.title: WA unions and state call for helping hand from federal government in Collie coal transition
item.description: The state government has already tipped $700 million into the transition of the WA town of Collie away from coal. Now, the Cook government wants tens of millions of federal dollars to finish the job.

item.title: Business says Labor's tax changes like a celebrity with 'too much plastic surgery'
item.description: Business has labelled Labor's proposed budget tax changes a "freak show", likening them to a celebrity who has had too much plastic surgery.

item.title: Ukraine's upgraded long-range drones a mounting headache for Moscow
item.description: Ukraine's growing arsenal of locally produced long-range drones are getting increasingly sophisticated, allowing Kyiv to increasingly strike targets across Moscow.

item.title: KPMG Australia under fire as parliamentary committee investigates whistleblower claims
item.description: Federal senators have accused senior leaders in Australia's accounting and auditing industry of behaving disgracefully as more revelations emerge about KPMG's whistleblower scandal.

item.title: Ravenswood Gold announces $1.4 billion refinancing of Queensland's largest mine
item.description: An expert has warned governments to keep an eye on gold mines experiencing financial troubles, given the surge in prices.

item.title: Woman comes to AFL great Tony Modra's aid after Fleurieu Peninsula truck crash
item.description: Linda Cameron describes how she rendered first aid to the former Crows player and held his hand as they waited for an ambulance following a truck crash in South Australia's Fleurieu Peninsula.

item.title: Timothy Loosemore found guilty of manslaughter over death of Maree Vermont in Goldie, Victoria
item.description: A jury has found Timothy Loosemore guilty of manslaughter over the death of Maree Vermont in Goldie, central Victoria.

item.title: Perth parents accused of starving daughter face retrial after court rules 'miscarriage of justice'
item.description: The WA Court of Appeal has overturned the guilty verdicts of Perth parents who starved their daughter after ruling that the judge's conduct in the jury trial amounted to a "miscarriage of justice".

item.title: Crucial miss sinks Dragons as Knights survive massive scare at home
item.description: The Newcastle Knights have provisionally moved into the NRL top four with a 22-20 win over the St George Illawarra Dragons. Follow live.

item.title: Gold drops 23pc from record high, miners drag ASX lower
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Hawks break drought at Carrara, winning tough encounter against Suns
item.description: The Hawks win at Carrara for the first time since 2014, after a tough, physical encounter against the Suns.

item.title: Meloni says Trump 'completely made up' claim she 'begged' him for a photo at G7
item.description: The Italian prime minister accused her one-time close ally of fabricating a story about her, after the US President told an Italian TV channel that she had "begged" him to take a photo with her at a G7 summit.

item.title: New anti-bullying reform in Malaysia could make parents liable for their children's behaviour
item.description: Malaysia has one of the highest rates of bullying in the world and new anti-bullying laws aim to tackle the problem.

item.title: Police release man arrested after three-year-old ends up in crocodile enclosure at UK zoo
item.description: The man, who was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after a three-year-old boy ended up in a zoo's crocodile enclosure, has been released on bail after police assessed him as not fit for an interview.

item.title: Australia wins T20 series with six-run win over Bangladesh in second game
item.description: Matt Renshaw blasted a career-best 89 not out as Australia notched a series-clinching six-run victory over Bangladesh in the second T20 of their three-match series.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree ceasefire as US-Iran deal under strain
item.description: Fighting escalated between Israel and Hezbollah in south Lebanon overnight, with reports of 47 people killed in Israeli strikes and four Israeli soldiers killed.

item.title: UK heading towards leadership contest after Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer's principal rival, Andy Burnham, has stormed back into parliament after a near-decade absence with a by-election win.

item.title: 'Sluggish' Socceroos fall to 2-0 defeat against USA
item.description: The Socceroos concede two first-half goals to fall to defeat against a fired-up United States in their crucial World Cup group stage game in Seattle.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Die Hard, terrifying tech and messy food
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: What Off Campus can teach us about centring women’s pleasure
item.description: The college hockey romance has had millions of people swooning over its main characters and the messages around healthy relationships.

item.title: How Donald Trump made peace with Iran's regime and the men Israel once set out to kill
item.description: When Iran and the United States inked a deal on the war this week, one unlikely figure on the Iranian side emerged from the shadows of the negotiations and into the open.

item.title: How One Nation turned a small country town orange
item.description: In just a few short weeks, a small country town became One Nation heartland. It is a blueprint for how the party can turn Australia orange.

item.title: Australian-first surgery allows baby twins who were born deaf to hear mum’s voice at the same time
item.description: An Australian-first eight-hour surgical feat had one main purpose — for Artie and Jack to hear their mum's voice at the same time.

item.title: How police are investigating who helped Dezi Freeman stay on the run
item.description: As police continue the investigation into Dezi Freeman's support network, experts say those who aided him might have shared similar ideology.

item.title: The Interview: Director Yeon Sang-ho says AI doesn't want our jobs, it wants our individuality
item.description: Train to Busan director Yeon Sang-ho is ringing the warning bell over AI's potential to strip humans of their individuality with his new zombie film Colony.

item.title: After fleeing the Taliban the Ganji sisters are on their way to Australian citizenship
item.description: Five years ago, Adiba was separated from her sister Arya as she fled Afghanistan. Now the Ganji siblings have reunited in Melbourne and are on their way to obtaining Australian citizenship.

item.title: Australia's first H5 bird flu case 'sobering but not unexpected'
item.description: Over recent years avian influenza has made its way across the globe. Now Australia has its first case. Here's what we know.

item.title: How blind faith in the internet helped Ash Jurberg fulfill a World Cup promise to his sons
item.description: The general advice is if it seems too good to be true, then it probably is. For Ash Jurberg, a punt on an anonymous message on LinkedIn sent him to the World Cup and fulfilled a 16-year-old promise to his sons.

item.title: Trump's limited capacity to sway Israel spells trouble for the Middle East
item.description: Just as Netanyahu's claims to be able to influence Trump have now become a burden to him, Trump's limited capacity to influence Netanyahu spells just as much trouble for peace in the Middle East.

item.title: Australian AI porn empire could be generating child abuse material
item.description: The ABC traced a Sydney startup to one of the world's biggest AI pornography sites and found what experts say is child abuse material.

item.title: Hanson's bulldozer moment could be a sign of new promiscuity in donor politics
item.description: One Nation's dramatic return to the national stage seems to have dashed any sense of restraint or shame about ties to wealthy donors.

item.title: What is 'monoculturalism' and what does it look like in practice?
item.description: Pauline Hanson has called for Australia to reject multiculturalism and be "monocultural". So what does it involve and are there any nations overseas that show what it might look like in Australia?

item.title: Micro flower farms popping up but pricing blooms is a challenge
item.description: Women looking for a new career or to invest in themselves are turning to micro flower farms, but pricing their blooms against industry giants can be challenging.

item.title: Gold Coast council reconsiders return of ocean pools
item.description: Its been decades since ocean pools had a presence along the Gold Coast shoreline. Now, council is considering whether they could make a comeback after strong community interest.

item.title: Crumptons' investment gives peanut growers renewed optimism after Bega closure
item.description: After being tossed a lifeline last year, Queensland's peanut harvest is in full swing with plans in place to help the industry thrive once again.

item.title: Farina's underground bakery volunteers reunite each year to serve up profits
item.description: When the alarm goes off at 3:30am in this freezing outback pit-stop, it's music to the ears of the volunteer bakers who happily spend their winters in a bakery like no other.

item.title: Langbroek tight-lipped over Aboriginal leadership changes dubbed 'Project Invisibility'
item.description: Under the direction of Arts Minister John-Paul Langbroek, eight Indigenous directors have disappeared from the boards of south-east Queensland's cultural institutions.

item.title: New owners of heritage Chelsea Farm in Baulkham Hills seek to unearth property's history
item.description: When the Tedesco family bought a sprawling property called Chelsea Farm in the Hills Shire district, they knew nothing of its more than 220-year history.

item.title: Alex de Minaur and Rinky Hijikata lose in Queen's Club Championships quarterfinals
item.description: Alex de Minaur and Rinky Hijikata both go down in the Queen's Club Championships quarterfinals as they prepare for Wimbledon.

item.title: Fans gather at live sites across the country as the Socceroos take on World Cup hosts USA
item.description: Thousands of people flocked to sites across Australia to show their support as the Socceroos fell short against World Cup host USA.

item.title: Superhero Day at Wagga schools for rare Schimke immuno-osseous dysplasia
item.description: Nate Lawson's family looked to their son's love of superheroes to raise awareness about the rare disease that took his life.

item.title: World Cup: Socceroos handed reality check by US and Tony Popovic has a week to find answers
item.description: Coming into the game on a high, the Socceroos copped a reality check in the first half against the USA. How they respond over the next week will define their 2026 World Cup campaign.

item.title: Paraguay star sent off under new rule as Türkiye is eliminated
item.description: A Paraguay star is sent off against Türkiye after covering his mouth during an altercation with an opponent.

item.title: The irresistible pull of the provocation: If you didn't do it at 40, do it at 60
item.description: For some women, the act of asking, "what might still be possible" — of refusing to accept that the most meaningful chapters are already behind them — might be its own kind of resistance.

item.title: If this is the better culture politicians promised, Tasmanian voters deserve an apology
item.description: After last year's snap election, Tasmania's premier declared voters were "sick and tired of the political games". After this week, it is clear that the politicians just cannot help themselves.

item.title: World Cup Socceroos quick hits: Nestory Irankunda starts on the bench and Cristian Volpato impresses
item.description: A Cameron Burgess own goal and VAR drama cost Australia against the US, before cramp proved a problem for the match referee. Here are the five quick hits from the Socceroos vs USA.

item.title: Indigenous language program aims to become Australia's first 'total immersion' school
item.description: After learning from Native American Yuchi families, Pertame elders and young mothers in Alice Springs are raising babies in a total immersion Language Nest and hope to extend the model into primary school.

item.title: AusAlert warning system testing begins in regional Australian communities
item.description: This week, phones in some regional towns have received a warning text alert and emitted an alarm as part of national warning system testing. Next month, your phone could receive it too.

item.title: Is the government's tax reform turning into a "freak show"? | Insiders On Background
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Warning issued over counterfeit weight-loss drugs labelled as retatrutide
item.description: Megan Hancocks bought a drug labelled as the peptide compound retatrutide in cash from a beautician. Within a week of her second dose, she was in acute liver failure.

item.title: First case of deadly H5 bird flu confirmed in Australia
item.description: The virus, found in a migratory bird in Esperance, raises concerns for the poultry industry despite Australia having spent years preparing for such a biosecurity breach.

item.title: World Cup: Socceroos legend Mark Schwarzer says Tony Popovic got his team selection 'desperately' wrong against USA
item.description: The Socceroos will rue a missed opportunity after a dire first half against the USA, with Tony Popovic's selections at least partly to blame, says Mark Schwarzer.

item.title: All Australian golfers fail to make the cut in 67-year US Open first
item.description: Cameron Smith, Adam Scott, Min Woo Lee and Lucas Herbert all fail to make it through to the weekend at the US Open.

item.title: Rainbow choir Pandemonium raises voices of LGBTQ+ community in Candelo, NSW
item.description: A choir formed in the tiny village of Candelo, on the New South Wales Far South Coast, is giving a voice to the LGBTQ+ community.

item.title: This warming apple and miso pudding will have you back for seconds - ABC News
item.description: Love a self-saucing pudding? This warm apple and miso version takes a classic to the next level

item.title: What is the bliss trade and has it elimated the risk of a share market crash?
item.description: Does "bliss" — big lasting state support for financial entities deemed too big to fail — make the stock market a one-way bet?

item.title: Australia Zoo calls police on traditional owner who came to see totem crocodile Old Faithful
item.description: Traditional owner Alwyn Lyall says he felt "extremely upset and humiliated" when police asked him to leave Australia Zoo, where he was trying to see a crocodile he says was "wrongfully arrested" and taken from the wild.

item.title: Transatlantic slavery was the gravest crime against humanity in history, Juneteenth marks its end
item.description: Americans are commemorating Juneteenth months after a UN resolution declaring the transatlantic slave trade the gravest crime against humanity.

item.title: Ratugolea hospitalised as Port suffer injury carnage in loss to Magpies
item.description: Port Adelaide defender Esava Ratugolea faces a lengthy stint on the sideline after being stretchered off with a suspected ruptured patellar tendon during the Power's 27-point loss to Collingwood on Saturday night.

item.title: Country music boom breathes new life into Australian festivals
item.description: The country music genre has grown in popularity since the pandemic, spawning huge hits and an equally huge following in 2026 that nobody saw coming.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: USA victory carries NFL flavour, Scotland criticises referee and Achraf Hakimi to stand trial
item.description: Scotland feels hard done by in their defeat against Morocco and a former NFL legend is on hand to photograph as the son of another of the NFL alumni cohort scores for his country. Here's the quick hits from the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Adelaide Crows fans wish Tony Modra well as they take on Melbourne at Adelaide Oval
item.description: Adelaide Crows fans wish the AFL great well in his recovery after surgery as former teammate Mark Ricciuto says he might not be as good-looking as he once was.

item.title: Drone footage of Dominican Republic resort fire.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Three arrested, four motorbikes seized after Mayfield gathering
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Brothers refused bail after allegedly evading police, participating in Mayfield riot
item.description: A court hears two brothers were tracked riding recklessly for 13km and attempted to evade police following a riot in Newcastle.

item.title: Crichton's golden point match-winner trumps Fozball in extra time
item.description: A Stephen Crichton field goal in golden point has delivered Canterbury a crucial upset win over Manly to keep their finals hopes alive.

item.title: One dead, 1,700 evacuated after fire in Dominican Republic resort
item.description: A woman has been killed and nearly 1,700 tourists evacuated due to a large fire at a hotel in the Dominican beach resort town of Bayahibe.

item.title: World Cup: Cristian Volpato is now bonded to the Socceroos, and that can only be good for Australia
item.description: Cristian Volpato only made a 30-minute cameo for the Socceroos in their 2-0 World Cup defeat against the USA, but in doing so showed his quality and desire to excel for Australia.

item.title: New World Cup laws get workout as Paraguay's Miguel Almirón sent off for covering mouth
item.description: Paraguay's Miguel Almirón fell victim to new laws put in place at this World Cup to counter discriminatory behaviour. So what are the new laws in place?

item.title: Beetlejuice the Musical's Australian run coming to an early close
item.description: Beetlejuice the Musical is calling curtains early on its Australian run, citing high operational costs.

item.title: Watchdog to consider complaints over NSW Police conduct at LGBTQIA+ venues in Sydney
item.description: Allegations of police aggression and intimidation during a drug operation at LGBTQIA+ venues across Sydney's Oxford Street will be considered by the law enforcement watchdog.

item.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency after weeks of protests
item.description: Bolivia's President Rodrigo Paz declares a state of emergency across the country, after more than six weeks of protests and road blockades.

item.title: Fuel excise rebate extended for another month, but at smaller discount
item.description: The federal government is winding back its discount to the fuel excise over the next month as the 32 cents per litre fuel discount will drop to 16 cents.

item.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz over Lebanon fighting, US says traffic still flowing
item.description: Iran says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz over Israel's continued attacks in Lebanon, which killed at least 16 people on Saturday, local time.

item.title: Australia remains unbeaten in Women's ICC T20 Cricket World Cup
item.description: Beth Mooney has played down any concerns about her back injury after Australia crushed the Netherlands by 98 runs in their T20 Women's World Cup clash in Southampton.

item.title: UK police probe cause of train collision that killed driver and left nine in critical condition
item.description: Nine people are in a critical condition after a collision between two passenger trains in central England.

item.title: Europe swelters under heatwave, prompting crisis talks in France
item.description: Much of Western Europe is sweltering in a heatwave with the mercury expected to continue rising in the coming days, likely shattering yet more temperature records.

item.title: Live: Germany rocked by Côte d'Ivoire, Netherlands thump Sweden
item.description: Germany face Côte d'Ivoire in Toronto after the Netherlands beat Sweden 5-1 to kick off a big day at the World Cup. Follow live.

item.title: Emma Louise on songwriting as salvation, and new album Sunshine For Happiness
item.description: It took seven years for the Jungle hit-maker and Flume collaborator to share the music that saved her following a "mental breakdown".

item.title: Did you pay attention at school? We'll find out with Guy Montgomery's Spelling Bee quiz
item.description: Think you can get 10 out of 10 on our spelling quiz? Just remember, autocorrect can't save you here.

item.title: Russia's Denis Pankratov shattered Scott Miller's Olympic dream in the 100m butterfly final at Atlanta 1996.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Scott Miller's three-decade fall from the Olympic dais ended in prison. It saved his life
item.description: From Olympic silver medallist to convicted drug supplier, Scott Miller opens up on one of the biggest downfalls in the history of Australian sport and the moments that saved his life as he seeks redemption.

item.title: Against an unfamiliar enemy, Labor is preparing a familiar campaign on health and child care
item.description: Pauline Hanson said thousands of words in her marathon appearance at the National Press Club, but only a few of them will be heard widely.

item.title: How the war in the Middle East is pushing tourists to Asia
item.description: The war in the Middle East has not stopped Australians travelling but their preferences may be changing.

item.title: Leigh Creek residents' vision for the town and the man who hopes to achieve it
item.description: A decade after the last coal truck rumbled out of Leigh Creek, Baljit Singh Bajwa put millions of dollars on the line to invest in the shrinking mining town.

item.title: A mass killing, bodies burnt and a 'conspiracy of silence': An outback massacre is not forgotten
item.description: Ronnie's great-grandfather collected bones and teeth from an outback creek bed. Now police say they're 'not proud' of what occurred.

item.title: West Papuan activists raise alarm over AI-generated deepfake videos
item.description: AI-generated deepfake videos of prominent West Papuan rights campaigners spark concerns about how disinformation is being used as a form of "digital colonisation".

item.title: Tehran has benefited from America's 'enemy' status. The truce with Trump could change that
item.description: For decades the Islamic republic has been using its "main enemy" America to distract from its repressive regime, analysts say. But the recent truce with Donald Trump could change that.

item.title: Where One Nation's foreign policy stands on Taiwan, foreign aid, the UN and 'more missiles'
item.description: With One Nation running red hot in the polls and posing a serious challenge to the incumbent parties, there's more scrutiny on what its foreign policy would mean for Australia.

item.title: Scottish counterterrorism police investigate Edinburgh attacks
item.description: Three of the five men injured during the attacks in the city's north and west require hospital treatment.
